decline-vol-i-ch-01-introduction.one_line: The whole thesis in miniature: history has a form, Cultures are organisms, and the West is in its winter.
decline-vol-i-ch-01-introduction.student: Spengler announces a science of history. Cultures, like plants, pass through spring, summer, autumn and winter and then harden into 'Civilization'. The familiar 'ancient–medieval–modern' scheme is a parochial Western conceit; in its place he offers a 'Copernican' view in which every Culture is one organism among several, each with its own life-cycle. Our own age, he argues, is the West's late autumn passing into winter.
decline-vol-i-ch-01-introduction.why_it_matters: Everything in the two volumes is an unfolding of the claims compressed into this chapter. Read it slowly and the rest of the book becomes a vast set of worked examples.

decline-vol-i-ch-02-the-meaning-of-numbe.one_line: Every Culture has its own mathematic; number is a symbol of the soul, not a universal truth.

decline-vol-i-ch-03-the-problem-of-world.one_line: Two ways of knowing: the systematic (Nature, causality) and the physiognomic (History, destiny).

decline-vol-i-ch-04-the-problem-of-world.one_line: Destiny is the logic of time; Causality the logic of space. History can only be felt, not calculated.

decline-vol-i-ch-05-makrokosmos-the-symb.one_line: A Culture's whole world grows from one root-intuition of space — its prime-symbol.

decline-vol-i-ch-06-makrokosmos-apollini.one_line: The three souls named: Apollinian (the body), Magian (the cavern), Faustian (infinite space).

decline-vol-i-ch-07-music-and-plastic-th.one_line: Each Culture chooses its arts: the statue for the Classical, the fugue and oil-painting for the West.

decline-vol-i-ch-08-music-and-plastic-ac.one_line: The nude and the portrait: Classical surface against Faustian depth and biography.

decline-vol-i-ch-09-soul-image-and-life-.one_line: Psychology is itself a Culture-form: each soul pictures itself in its own image.

decline-vol-i-ch-10-soul-image-and-life-.one_line: Buddhism, Stoicism and Socialism: the 'contemporary' final moralities of three Cultures.

decline-vol-i-ch-11-faustian-and-apollin.one_line: Even physics is a faith: each Culture's science is a myth of its own soul, and ours dissolves into pure function.

decline-vol-ii-ch-01-origin-and-landscap.one_line: Life in two keys: the cosmic 'being' that pulses in the blood, and the waking-being of the free-moving microcosm.

decline-vol-ii-ch-02-origin-and-landscap.one_line: Why the high Cultures are so few, how they stand together as a group, and what 'world-history' really surveys.

decline-vol-ii-ch-03-origin-and-landscap.one_line: How Cultures meet, borrow and deform one another — the germ of the 'pseudomorphosis' worked out later.

decline-vol-ii-ch-04-cities-and-peoples-.one_line: World-history is the history of the city: the city turns peasant into citizen, and at last into the nomad of the metropolis.

decline-vol-ii-ch-05-cities-and-peoples-.one_line: Race, people and tongue are not biology but destiny — what a landscape and a history stamp on a stream of being.

decline-vol-ii-ch-06-cities-and-peoples-.one_line: Before and after a Culture: the primitive peoples that come before it, and the 'fellaheen' who outlive it.

decline-vol-ii-ch-07-problems-of-the-ara.one_line: When a young soul is forced into the alien mould of an older Culture — the Magian cramped beneath Classical forms.

decline-vol-ii-ch-08-problems-of-the-ara.one_line: The Magian world: the cavern-feeling, the divine substance, the consensus of the faithful — the soul behind the great religions.

decline-vol-ii-ch-09-problems-of-the-ara.one_line: Religion in the springtime of three Cultures — Classical cult, Magian revelation, Faustian Puritanism.

decline-vol-ii-ch-10-the-state-the-probl.one_line: The great estates — nobility and priesthood, time against space, the manor against the cathedral — as a Culture's living form.

decline-vol-ii-ch-11-the-state-state-and.one_line: The state as a people held 'in form': the history-making minority, the political stream, and the meaning of the great event.

decline-vol-ii-ch-12-the-state-philosoph.one_line: What it means to be 'in form' politically — the statesman against the masses, and the state's slow sinking into mere economics.

decline-vol-ii-ch-13-the-form-world-of-e.one_line: Money as a Faustian way of thinking — abstract, dynamic — that turns the world into book-keeping and dissolves the old estates.

decline-vol-ii-ch-14-the-form-world-of-e.one_line: The machine as the Faustian will-to-power made iron: the engineer, the entrepreneur, and the gathering revolt against the machine.

man-technics-ch-01-technics-as-the-tacti.one_line: Technics is not the tool but the tactics of living — the whole way a being wages its war with the world.

man-technics-ch-02-herbivores-and-beasts.one_line: Two forms of life: the herbivore that flees and the beast of prey that hunts — and man, by his soul, is a beast of prey.

man-technics-ch-03-the-origin-of-man-han.one_line: The hand and the tool make man: the free hand that seizes a weapon turns the beast of prey into the maker.

man-technics-ch-04-the-second-stage-spee.one_line: Speech and the deed-in-common: work is organized, the 'enterprise' is born, and the leaders divide from the led.

man-technics-ch-05-the-last-act-rise-and.one_line: The Faustian machine-culture masters the world — and races toward its own exhaustion and end.
